description: Implement the idle-research swarm per ADR-2604151200. P1 wires the idle-trigger + throttle into the sched daemon and writes the structured-finding schema. P2 implements the deterministic (code-first) analysts. P3 wires the T2.5 LLM synthesis analysts. P4 routes findings back into the draft ADR/workplan/memory surfaces. P5 surfaces sweep state on the dashboard.

tasks.title: Add idle_tick_count tracking to queue_drain — increment when pending=0 and in_flight=0, reset otherwise. Threshold default 4 ticks (configurable via .hex/project.json sched.idle_threshold_ticks).
Self-enqueue 'research-sweep' kind when idle threshold reached AND last_sweep older than min_sweep_interval_h (default 6h, persisted at ~/.hex/sched/last_research_sweep).

Define the structured-finding YAML schema in hex-core/src/research_finding.rs — Finding { id, domain, severity, title, evidence[], suggested_action: { kind, draft_ref } }. Include serde_yaml round-trip test.
Unit test: idle-trigger fires after N=4 consecutive empty ticks, does NOT fire when last_sweep < 6h ago, DOES fire when last_sweep ≥ 6h ago.
Architecture analyst: shell out to `hex analyze . --json`, parse violations + dead-code into Finding records (domain=architecture, severity from analyze output).
Code-quality analyst: run `cargo check --workspace --message-format=json` and `cargo clippy --workspace --message-format=json`, parse warnings/errors into Finding records.
Drift analyst: shell out to `hex plan reconcile --all --dry-run --json`, parse mismatches (status drift, missing files) into Finding records.

Performance analyst: collect hot-path heuristics (functions > 200 LoC, deeply nested loops, non-async I/O in async contexts) via tree-sitter, then T2.5 (devstral-small-2:24b) synthesizes top 3 actionable Finding records with concrete file:line evidence.
Naming-convention analyst: tree-sitter walk public symbols across all crates, T2 model checks for inconsistencies (snake_case vs camelCase drift, abbreviation use, non-Rust-idiomatic names).
UX analyst: walk hex-nexus/assets/ dashboard routes, run accessibility/consistency checks (color tokens, font usage, error states), T2.5 synthesizes top findings.

phases.title: T2.5 LLM-synthesis analysts (gated behind code-first findings)

tasks.title: Sweep coordinator: invoke all enabled analysts, collect Findings, write docs/analysis/idle-sweep-YYYYMMDD-HHMM.yaml + a rendered .md summary. Cap at max_drafts_per_sweep=5 (configurable).
For each Finding with suggested_action.kind=adr, write a draft under docs/adrs/drafts/ADR-<finding-id>.md using the existing ADR template. Drafts are NEVER auto-promoted to Accepted.

For each Finding with suggested_action.kind=workplan, write a draft JSON under docs/workplans/drafts/ using the existing workplan schema. For kind=memory, call hex_memory_store with namespace='idle-sweep'.
Preemption: if queue_drain finds a non-research task while a sweep is in-flight, signal-abort the sweep cleanly and re-enqueue research-sweep for the next idle window.

phases.title: Route findings back into ADR/workplan/memory drafts

tasks.title: Add 'last_sweep: <age> (<n> findings, <m> drafts)' to `hex sched daemon status` output and `hex` no-arg status panel.
Dashboard panel: GET /api/research/sweeps returns last N sweep summaries (timestamp, finding count, draft count, links to drafts). Solid.js component renders as a card on the main dashboard.
